channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ekurhuleni Municipality Addresses Sewer Blockages in Villa Liza
item.description: Residents of Villa Liza and Sikhakhane Street in Jabavu, Soweto, have been dealing with recurring sewer overflow issues for three years, with no permanent solution. The City of Ekurhuleni attributes t...

item.title: Google's Stock Performance and Market Implications
item.description: The article discusses recent fluctuations in Google's stock price and their potential impact on investors. Analysts highlight concerns about the company's advertising revenue growth, which has slowed ...

item.title: Local Governments Strengthen Maritime Sovereignty in West Philippine Sea Through Governance Initiatives

item.title: Top Fruits for Enhancing Brain Health and Cognitive Function
item.description: This article explores the nutritional benefits of five fruits linked to improved brain health, based on expert research. Blueberries, rich in anthocyanins, may enhance memory and blood flow. Citrus fr...

item.title: GLP-1 Medications and the Risk of Yo-Yo Dieting Cycles
item.description: The article discusses the potential for weight-loss medications like Wegovy and Mounjaro to create a new cycle of yo-yo dieting. While these drugs, which mimic hormones to reduce appetite and increase...

item.title: Trump's Frustration Over Prolonged Iran Conflict
item.description: President Donald Trump reportedly unleashed a profanity-laden temper tantrum in the Oval Office over his failure to end the Iran war, according to an inside source. The incident, detailed in a Wall St...

item.title: Murphy Reid becomes youngest Fremantle player to win Glendinning-Allan Medal after derby triumph
item.description: Murphy Reid, 19, has made history by becoming the youngest player in Fremantle's AFL history to win the prestigious Glendinning-Allan Medal. The young star delivered a standout performance in Fremantl...

item.title: Madlanga Commission Deliberates on Suliman Carrim's Postponement Request

item.title: Canada’s Skilled Military Recruit Pathway Draws Few Invitations

item.title: Wildfire Smoke Continues to Impact Kelowna
item.description: Wildfire smoke has been persistently affecting the region of Kelowna, with residents experiencing poor air quality and health concerns. The article highlights the ongoing challenges posed by the smoke...

item.title: Record Number of Migrants Reach UK in Single Boat Amid Channel Crisis
item.description: A record 165 migrants have reached the UK in a single boat, breaking the previous Channel crossing record of 128 set in July. The Home Office confirmed the arrival, highlighting the dangers faced by t...

item.title: US imposes tariffs on 60 countries over forced labor allegations
item.description: The United States has imposed new tariffs of 10% and 12.5% on goods from 60 trading partners, including the EU and China, citing failures to curb imports linked to forced labor. This move follows the ...

item.description: The Philippine Army has arrested two suspected members of the Bangsamoro Islamic Freedom Fighters-Dawlah Islamiya (BIFF-DI) group in Maguindanao del Sur, following a joint operation with local authori...

item.title: Rahul Gandhi accuses government of misleading on pellet gun use during protests; HC rejects NIA inquiry

item.title: Quebec OB-GYN Association Challenges Pay Gap in Legal Action
item.description: The Quebec Obstetricians and Gynecologists Association has filed a legal challenge against the Federation of Medical Specialists, alleging a persistent gender-related pay gap. Despite overall increase...

item.title: Jon Bon Jovi Reschedules MSG Show After Vocal Issues During Performance
item.description: Jon Bon Jovi abruptly ended his Madison Square Garden concert on July 23, 2026, due to vocal issues, informing fans he would reschedule the show. The incident occurred two weeks into his 'Bon Jovi For...
